title: quercus × stelloides
intro: Quercus × stelloides, also known as the hybrid white oak, is a rare deciduous tree that is a natural hybrid of the Quercus stellata and Quercus coccinea species. The bark of the Quercus × stelloides tree contains tannins, which have been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus × stelloides include potential uses in treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as acting as a natural antiseptic and astringent. Quercus × stelloides can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
